originalTitle: Here’s what Samsung’s smart glasses actually look like
summary: Samsung has given us our first chance to check out its upcoming smart glasses in person, revealing two new designs and the first specs in the process, including an impressive 9-hour battery life. The glasses, developed in collaboration with Google and the eyewear brands Gentle Monster and Warby Parker, are due to launch this fall. […]
content: Samsung has given us our first chance to check out its upcoming smart glasses in person, revealing two new designs and the first specs in the process, including an impressive 9-hour battery life. The glasses, developed in collaboration with Google and the eyewear brands Gentle Monster and Warby Parker, are due to launch this fall. Google and Samsung have been teasing the new glasses for some time, and revealed the first two designs at I/O in May. Now there are two more frames: Both are unsurprisingly boxy, with a new set of squared off black glasses from Gentle Monster, and a more rounded, red-brown pair from Warby Parker. Jaein Choi, the executive vice president running Samsung’s mobile experience division, told CNBC that there are “more designs” on the way. This was our first chance to see any of the four frames in person, though Samsung wouldn’t let me try them on — or even hold them up to my face. They’re all impressively light — not much heavier than the thick-rimmed acrylic glasses I used to wear, and barely bigger than them either. Speaker and microphone grilles are hidden along the stems, a single button sits on the right with a power switch tucked inside on the left, and of course there’s a camera and recording LED mounted on the front. Much like Meta’s collaborations with Ray-Ban and Oakley, these basically look like normal glasses most people would happily wear — privacy connotations aside. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-06.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-11.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-09.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-07.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-10.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-04.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-05.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-03.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-02.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-01.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-12.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-13.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] The new glasses will be powered by Qualcomm’s Snapdragon AR1 Gen 1 chip, the same silicon that powers Meta’s latest smart glasses. Samsung says they’ll run for up to nine hours on a single charge, while the accompanying charging case delivers up to seven additional charges.  You’ll be able to use either Google Gemini or Samsung’s Bixby with the glasses, though the AI features themselves sound pretty familiar: message summaries, live translation, navigation guidance, and analysis or description of what’s captured by the cameras. Speaking of, just like Meta’s, there is a recording LED for when the camera is capturing video. Choi told Bloomberg that, like Meta’s, the glasses will prevent recording if they detect the LED has been blocked or obscured. That hasn’t been enough to prevent Meta’s from being branded “pervert glasses,” so we’ll have to see if Samsung and Google have other plans for tackling what Choi called an “an industry-shared problem.” Samsung still isn’t revealing a firm release date or price for the upcoming glasses, but Choi described them as a “premium” product.
chineseTitle: 这是三星智能眼镜的实际外观
chineseSummary: 三星让我们第一次有机会亲眼目睹其即将推出的智能眼镜，并展示了两种新设计和首批规格，其中包括令人印象深刻的 9 小时电池续航时间。这款眼镜是与谷歌以及眼镜品牌 Gentle Monster 和 Warby Parker 合作开发的，将于今年秋季推出。 […]
chineseContent: 三星让我们第一次有机会亲眼目睹其即将推出的智能眼镜，并展示了两种新设计和首批规格，其中包括令人印象深刻的 9 小时电池续航时间。这款眼镜是与谷歌以及眼镜品牌 Gentle Monster 和 Warby Parker 合作开发的，将于今年秋季推出。谷歌和三星已经预告这款新眼镜有一段时间了，并在 5 月份的 I/O 大会上公布了前两款设计。现在又有两款镜框：两款镜架都是四四方方的，一副来自 Gentle Monster 的新方形黑色眼镜，一副更圆润的红棕色眼镜来自 Warby Parker。三星移动体验部门执行副总裁 Jaein Choi 告诉 CNBC，“更多设计”正在酝酿之中。这是我们第一次有机会亲眼目睹这四个框架中的任何一个，尽管三星不让我试戴它们，甚至不让我把它们举到我面前。它们都非常轻——比我以前戴的厚框亚克力眼镜重不了多少，也比它们大不了多少。扬声器和麦克风格栅隐藏在立柱上，右侧有一个按钮，左侧有一个电源开关，当然前面还安装了一个摄像头和录音 LED。就像 Meta 与雷朋 (Ray-Ban) 和奥克利 (Oakley) 的合作一样，这些基本上看起来就像大多数人乐意佩戴的普通眼镜——抛开隐私含义不谈。 [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-06.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [图片： https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/samsung-google-gentle-monster-warby-parker-ai-glasses-11.jpg?quality=90&strip=all] [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
